description: Four neural-network tasks — MLP, Naive Bayes NN, Linear Regression NN, and Logistic Regression NN — each implemented in PyTorch with self-verifying exit codes.

tasks.name: Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) on MNIST
tasks.description: Three-hidden-layer fully-connected MLP (784→256→128→64→10) trained on MNIST handwritten digits. Uses ReLU activations, Dropout (0.2), Adam optimiser, and StepLR scheduler.

tasks.name: Naive Bayes as Neural Network on 20 Newsgroups
tasks.description: Naive Bayes expressed as a single linear layer whose weights represent learned log-likelihoods (kept non-negative via softplus re-parameterisation) and whose bias represents the learned log-prior. Trained on TF-IDF bag-of-words features from the 20 Newsgroups corpus using Adam + NLL loss, which recovers the MLE/MAP solution analytically computed by classical Naive Bayes.

tasks.name: Linear Regression as Neural Network on California Housing
tasks.description: Ordinary least-squares linear regression expressed as a single nn.Linear layer (no activation). Trained with MSELoss and SGD + momentum + CosineAnnealingLR on the California Housing dataset (StandardScaler preprocessing). Recovers the OLS solution via gradient descent.

tasks.name: Logistic Regression as Neural Network on Breast Cancer Wisconsin
tasks.description: Binary logistic regression expressed as a single nn.Linear layer whose raw logits feed BCEWithLogitsLoss (numerically stable). L2 regularisation (weight_decay) implements the Ridge / MAP penalty. An additional non-triviality check ensures the model predicts both classes (not degenerate). Reports both accuracy and AUC-ROC.

exit_codes.0: PASS — accuracy >= 0.93 AND AUC-ROC >= 0.97 AND both classes predicted
